subject: Ecology
title: Salt Marshes
description: A quick win: understand Salt Marshes.

hook.text: Salt marshes hug the coastline, flooded twice daily by tides, and are among Earth's most productive ecosystems.

buildup.text: Cordgrass and pickleweed tolerate salt that kills most plants, dominating the tidal zone.

discovery.text: Dead cordgrass becomes detritus—the base of a food web feeding shrimp, crabs, and fish.

twist.text: Salt marshes buffer coastlines from storm surges better than seawalls—and they repair themselves.

climax.text: They also bury carbon at rates 10 to 50 times faster than tropical forests per unit area.

punchline.text: Mud, grass, and tides—Earth's most efficient carbon sink.

quiz.question: How fast do salt marshes bury carbon compared to tropical forests?
options: 10 to 50 times faster per unit area
